calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Off Calendar

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Robert Philip Sprague, Esq. (415-932-8977), counsel for plaintiffs/applicant parties Daniel P. McLoughlin, Esq. (510-845-1816), counsel for defendant/opposing party Sequoia Mortgage

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden. Plaintiffs fail to sustain their burden and show a possibility of prevailing on the merits and irreparable harm. Plaintiffs do not submit competent evidence that Myers spent the money ($300,000) on construction and, but for Fried's non-cooperation, would have gotten the money from the construction Disbursement Account, and used it to pay mortgage. There are no receipts, cancelled checks, permits, DBI approvals, etc. Plaintiffs also do not produce any evidence that these repairs were done and reimbursement could have been requested before the loan went into default in December 2013. Also, there is no showing of irreparable harm. Mr. Myers admits that he wanted to sell the property for years, thus, the sole proposition in support of showing of irreparable harm, i.e. that that real property is unique, is irrelevant in this case. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
